    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "/**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": " * Update position history table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": " */"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "function updateTablePosition(data) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "    const table = document.getElementById('positionHistoryTable');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    if (!table) return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "    // Update headers"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "    const headerRow = table.querySelector('thead tr');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "    if (headerRow) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "        headerRow.innerHTML = '\u003cth\u003eTeam\u003c/th\u003e';"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "        data.weeks.forEach(week =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "            headerRow.innerHTML += &#96;\u003cth colspan=\"2\"\u003eWeek ${week}\u003c/th\u003e&#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "        });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "    // Add subheader row for Points/Average"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "    const existingSubheader = table.querySelector('thead tr:nth-child(2)');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "    if (existingSubheader) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "        existingSubheader.remove();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    const subheaderRow = document.createElement('tr');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    subheaderRow.innerHTML = '\u003cth\u003e\u003c/th\u003e';  // Empty cell for team name"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "    data.weeks.forEach(() =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "        subheaderRow.innerHTML += '\u003cth\u003ePoints\u003c/th\u003e\u003cth\u003eAverage\u003c/th\u003e';"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "    const thead = table.querySelector('thead');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "    if (thead) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "        thead.appendChild(subheaderRow);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "    // Update body"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "    const tbody = table.querySelector('tbody');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "    if (tbody) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "        tbody.innerHTML = '';"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "        data.data.forEach(teamData =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "            const row = document.createElement('tr');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "            row.innerHTML = &#96;\u003ctd\u003e${teamData.team}\u003c/td\u003e&#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "            "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "            teamData.weeks.forEach(week =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "                row.innerHTML += &#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "                    \u003ctd\u003e${week.points.toFixed(1)}\u003c/td\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "                    \u003ctd\u003e${week.average.toFixed(2)}\u003c/td\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "                &#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "            });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "            "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "            tbody.appendChild(row);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "        });"
          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "function renderTeamVsTeamComparison(data, options) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "${getText('ui.heatmap.high', 'High:')}\u003c/span\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "                                            \u003cdiv class=\"heat-map-legend\" style=\"background: ${window.ColorUtils?.getThemeColor('heatMapHigh') || '#1b8da7'}; width: 20px; height: 20px;\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "                                        \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "                                        \u003csmall class=\"text-muted\"\u003e${getText('ui.range_label', 'Range:')} ${data.metadata?.points_range?.min || 'N/A'} - ${data.metadata?.points_range?.max || 'N/A'}\u003c/small\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "                                    \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "                                \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "                            \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "                        \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "                    \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "                \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "        \u003c/div\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "    &#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "    // Store column metadata for filtering"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "    const columnMetadata = extractColumnMetadata(data);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "    // Create table using Tabulator"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "        const tableOptions = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "            disablePositionCircle,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "            tooltips: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "            enableHeatMap"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "        };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "        if (teamField) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "            tableOptions.teamField = teamField;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "        createTableTabulator(tableId, data, tableOptions);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "        // Attach filter listeners and apply default filter after table is created"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "        setTimeout(() =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "            attachTeamComparisonFilterListeners(tableId, columnMetadata);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "            applyTeamComparisonFilter(tableId, 'points', columnMetadata);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "        }, 200);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "        // Fallback to Bootstrap"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, data, {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "            disablePositionCircle,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "            enableHeatMap"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "        });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "    } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "        console.error('Table creation function not available');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "/**"
          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "/**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": " * Renders the team vs team comparison card and table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": " * @param {Object} data - Team vs team comparison data from API"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": " * @param {Object} options - Rendering options"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": " * @param {string} options.containerId - ID of the container element to render into"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": " * @param {string} options.tableId - ID for the table element (will be created inside container)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": " * @param {string} [options.headerLevel='h6'] - HTML header level ('h5', 'h6', etc.)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": " * @param {boolean} [options.disablePositionCircle=false] - Whether to disable position circles"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": " * @param {boolean} [options.enableHeatMap=true] - Whether to enable heat map"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": " * @param {string} [options.teamField] - Field name for team identification (for position circles)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": " */"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "function renderTeamVsTeamComparison(data, options) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    const {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "        containerId,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "        tableId,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "        headerLevel = 'h6',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "        disablePositionCircle = false,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "        enableHeatMap = true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "        teamField = null"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    } = options;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "        console.warn('No team vs team comparison data available');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "        return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "    if (!container) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "        console.error(&#96;Team vs team comparison container not found: ${containerId}&#96;);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "        return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "    // Get translation function if available"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "    const t = typeof window.t === 'function' ? window.t : null;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "    const getText = (key, fallback) =\u003e (t ? t(key, fallback) : fallback);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "    // Use title and description from API response (which includes proper i18n with article_male + season/week)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "    // The backend provides: title with i18n, and description with \"team_vs_team_comparison_matrix_explanation\" + article_male + season/week"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    const title = data.title || getText('team_vs_team_comparison_matrix', 'Team vs Team Comparison Matrix');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    const description = data.description || getText('ui.team_vs_team.description', 'Matrix showing team performance against each opponent');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "    // Translation labels for filter buttons"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

          },
          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "async loadGameOverviewData(state) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "const container = ('tableGameOverview');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "            if (container) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "                // Use Tabulator for the game overview table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "                if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "                    console.log('Using createTableTabulator function for game overview table');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "                    createTableTabulator('tableGameOverview', tableData, { "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "                        disablePositionCircle: false, // Enable position circles for team and opponent positions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "                        tooltips: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "                        enableHeatMap: true // Enable heatmap coloring for pins and points columns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "                    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "                } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "                    console.log('Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 function');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "                    createTableBootstrap3('tableGameOverview', tableData, { "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "                        disablePositionCircle: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "                    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "                } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "                    console.error('No table creation function available');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "                    container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "                }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "        } catch (error) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "            console.error('Error loading game overview:', error);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "            const container = document.getElementById('tableGameOverview');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "            if (container) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "                container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('error_loading_data', 'Error loading game overview') : 'Error loading game overview'}: ${error.message || error}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "            }"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "class LeagueComparisonBlock extends BaseContentBlock {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "    /**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "     * Render the comparison table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "     */"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "    renderComparisonTable(data) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "        const tableContainer = document.getElementById('tableLeagueComparison');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "        if (!tableContainer) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "            console.warn('Table container not found, skipping table render');"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "            return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "        const seasons = Object.keys(data).sort();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        // Create comparison table data"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see a critical bug where the condition is inverted. The logic is inverted: when group titles exist, it flattens the structure; when titles are missing, it preserves the grouped structure. This means:

- When `hasGroupTitles` is true (some group has a title), it goes to the "flatten" path
- When `hasGroupTitles` is false (no groups have titles), it keeps the normal grouped structure

This is backwards from what's expected. Since the user's data includes group titles, `hasGroupTitles` is true, triggering the nested else branch starting at line 684+. This branch processes `group.columns.map`, but can return null when `columnInfo` is missing, potentially breaking Tabulator's column definitions.

The `columnLookup` is constructed from `columnOrder`, which originates from `flattenColumnMetadata`. Keys in this lookup follow the format `${groupIndex}-${columnIndex}` for every column in order.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The grep command failed on the vendor tabulator, possibly due to a different path. Let me glob tabulator.

Key issue: `games = sorted(player_data[Columns.round_number].unique())`

If `round_number` is **nullable Int64** or **object** with mix of int and str, `== game` comparison in:
```python
player_rows[player_rows[Columns.round_number] == game]
```
might fail to match for some rows when `game` is numpy.int64(1) but column has Python int 1 - usually works in pandas.

- Duplicate games in unique(): numpy float64 1.0 and int64 1 - `np.unique` might collapse but pandas `.unique()` on a Series with mixed types could give [1, 1.0] as two elements!
- Let me verify: When `round_number` is nullable Int64 or object with mixed types, comparisons like `== game` may fail if `game` is `numpy.int64(1)` but the column contains Python int 1.

- `np.unique` may collapse duplicates like `1` and `1.0`, while `pandas.Series.unique()` might treat them as separate values.
- Column names generated using f-strings with `numpy.int64` produce valid names (e.g., `"game1_score"`), but `numpy.float64(1.0)` creates invalid names like `"game1.0_score"` which can break CSS selectors or other tools expecting clean identifiers.

This inconsistency can cause issues during data processing or field name generation. Field names with dots (`.`) in Tabulator are interpreted as nested object paths, which can cause issues when field names like `game1.0_score` are used. This leads Tabulator to attempt accessing nested properties such as `row.game1[0][_score]`, resulting in undefined values and empty cells. Old data with integer round numbers creates clean field names like `game1_score`, but when round numbers are floats (e.g., 1.0), field names become `game1.0_score`, which Tabulator interprets as nested paths, causing empty cells.

**Fix**: Normalize game numbers to integers or strings without dots when generating field names in `league_service.py`:

- Convert float to int if possible.
- Replace dots with underscores as fallback. When iterating over games, use integers for keys or normalized strings for field names to ensure consistency.

For filtering, the comparison `player_rows[player_rows[Columns.round_number] == game]` should work as long as `game` is from a normalized `games` list.

Use normalized game numbers for field names:  
- `field=f"game{_normalized_round_for_field(game)}_score"`

Normalize the `games` list to sorted unique integers to prevent duplicate columns caused by 1 and 1.0.  
- Convert float values to int when possible.  
- Fall back to replacing dots with underscores if needed.</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
Pipeline CSVs (via pandas) often store **Round Number** as **floats** (`1.0`, `2.0`, …). The team-week table used those values in column **field** names, e.g. `f"game{game}_score"` → **`game1.0_score`**.

Tabulator’s default **`nestedFieldSeparator` is `"."`**, so it treats that string as a **nested path**, not a single property. The row object has flat keys like `game1.0_score` from the API, but Tabulator looks up **`game["1"]["0_score"]`** (or similar), gets **undefined**, and the **score/points cells look empty**. Legacy data used integer rounds, so you got **`game1_score`** and everything worked.

### Fix
In **`get_team_week_details_table_data`** (`league_service.py`):

1. Build **`games`** as sorted **ints** from `pd.to_numeric(..., errors="coerce")`, so column fields are always **`game1_score`**, **`game2_score`**, …
2. Add **`_matches_round(df, game_int)`** so filters use numeric equality (`1` matches `1.0` in the frame).

No change is required on the frontend; reload the league page and open the same team/week again.

If the **head-to-head (“new full”)** view ever looks empty for the same reason, we can normalize rounds there too (and sanitize player names with `.` in field keys); say if you want that in the same pass.

---

_**User**_

since recently, some games are not imported properly, e.g. BayL, Comet, Week 4 only has 4*8 games instead of 4*9
Week 3 for Comet only has 4*7 games. double check if they are in the fechted data, and where in the process they get lost

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The dedupe key consists only of `(Game-ID, Teamname)` and does not include player slot or Spiel-ID differences. Each canonical row represents one team's sheet for a game with four players in columns Spieler 1-4, not four separate rows. Therefore, dedupe correctly retains one row per team per game.

The discrepancy "4*8 vs 4*9" indicates eight or nine missing games, implying entire Game-ID rows are absent from the canonical merge, not dropped player lines during dedupe.

Possible causes:
- Incremental fetch using `since_date_updated` may miss entries not updated when others were
- staging_row_to_canonical may drop rows
- sanitize_rows may drop rows</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Found important difference:

- sanitize_rows uses key `(game_id, team, source_entry_id)`
- Keeps multiple rows if different entry IDs exist for the same game+team combination
- This behavior is unusual but acceptable The dedupe_source_rows function in the merge step uses only (Game-ID, Teamname) as its key, excluding source_entry_id. This means that if the same game and team appear as separate entriessuch as corrections or duplicates from different formsonly one row is retained based on timestamp.

This behavior is intentional for handling duplicate perspectives in Re/_Li but may mistakenly merge two valid rows if they share the same Game-ID and Teamname incorrectly.

Duplicate validation typically requires that same games from different perspectives use identical Game-ID for the same team.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The adapter maps numeric field IDs to their canonical forms. The adapter maps numeric field IDs to canonical forms, where field "1" maps to "Spiel-ID" and field "57" maps to "Game-ID".  
- The deduplication process uses `row.get("Game-ID")`, not "Spiel-ID".
- If only "Spiel-ID" is populated and "Game-ID" is empty, all rows may collapse to the same key ("", "Teamname").
- This would cause all teams across all games to merge into one row per team name, which is catastrophic. If Game-ID is empty but Spiel-ID is populated, all rows from the same team could collide under the same key ("", "BC Comet Nrnberg"), causing all games for that team to collapse into one row per deduplication round.

This would result in only one game per team being retained in the merged canonical data, explaining why some games appear to be missing.

**Fix**: Use `Game-ID or Spiel-ID` when constructing the deduplication key. For `row_effective_timestamp`, the row might still contain valid date information.

Also verify that sanitization logic correctly handles both fields: `row.get("Game-ID") or row.get("Spiel-ID")`  this looks correct. The bug occurs because **dedupe_source_rows** only considers **Game-ID**, while the canonical adapter maps field "1" to **Spiel-ID** and field "57" to **Game-ID**. If **Game-ID** is empty or duplicated, **Spiel-ID** should be used as a fallback for deduplication.

To fix this:
- Update `dedupe_source_rows` to use **Game-ID or Spiel-ID** for the deduplication key.
- Ensure sanitization logic correctly handles both fields: `row.get("Game-ID") or row.get("Spiel-ID")`.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So parse_game_id regex: `^([A-Z]+(?:[A-Z0-9])*)` The regex `^([A-Z]+(?:[A-Z0-9])*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$` extracts game IDs like `BAYL-M-691`, where:
- Group 1: `BAYL-M` (team identifier)
- Group 2: `691` (week, round, match)

For `BAYL-M-691`:
- week = triple[0] = '6'
- round_number = triple[1] = '9'
- match_number = 1 (zero-based = 0)

This suggests week 6, but legacy data shows week 4 for BayL games, indicating possible inconsistency in naming conventions. The parsed week 6 for Game-ID BAYL-M-691 contradicts legacy data showing week 4. The regex extracts week from the first digit of the triple (e.g., 691  '6'), but legacy data indicates week 4 for BayL games in 25/26 season.

- For week 4, the triple should start with '4' (e.g., 401, 412)
- Current ID BAYL-M-691 yields week 6, not 4
- Legacy data shows BayL played week 4 in 25/26 season
- This discrepancy suggests either:
  - Different Game-ID encoding for week 4
  - Or an error in current parsing logic</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Counting rows at each pipeline stage for BC Comet Nürnberg, BayL, weeks 3 and 4.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer" && python -c "
import csv, json, glob, os
from collections import defaultdict

def parse_gid(gid):
    import re
    m = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+(?:-[A-Z0-9]+)*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$', (gid or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    triple = m.group(2)
    return triple[0], triple[1], int(triple[2])

TEAM = 'BC Comet Nürnberg'
LEAGUES = {'BayL', 'BAYL'}

def count_canonical(path, label):
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        print(label, 'MISSING')
        return
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        r = csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')
        rows = [x for x in r if TEAM in (x.get('Teamname') or '')]
    by_w = defaultdict(list)
    for x in rows:
        gid = (x.get('Game-ID') or '').strip()
        sid = (x.get('Spiel-ID') or '').strip()
        p = parse_gid(gid)
        if p:
            wk = p[0]
            by_w[wk].append((gid, sid, x.get('source_entry_id')))
    print('---', label, '---')
    for w in sorted(by_w.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
        gids = set(t[0] for t in by_w[w])
        print(f'  parsed_week_digit={w!r}: rows={len(by_w[w])} distinct_Game-ID={len(gids)}')

# merged canonical
count_canonical('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv', 'merged_gf_canonical')

# latest legacy
path = 'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/legacy_out/latest.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    r = csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')
    leg = [x for x in r if TEAM in (x.get('Team') or '') and (x.get('League') or '') in LEAGUES]
by_w = defaultdict(set)
for x in leg:
    wk = (x.get('Week') or '').strip()
    rn = (x.get('Round Number') or '').strip()
    mn = (x.get('Match Number') or '').strip()
    if x.get('Player') or '').strip() == 'Team Total':
        by_w[wk].add((rn, mn))
print('--- legacy latest (Team Total rows = matches) ---')
for w in sorted(by_w.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
    print(f'  Week={w!r}: matches={len(by_w[w])}')
"
</summary>

from collections import defaultdict

    import re
    m = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+(?:-[A-Z0-9]+)*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$', (gid or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    triple = m.group(2)
    return triple[0], triple[1], int(triple[2])

TEAM = 'BC Comet Nürnberg'
LEAGUES = {'BayL', 'BAYL'}

    if not os.path.exists(path):
        print(label, 'MISSING')
        return
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        r = csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')
        rows = [x for x in r if TEAM in (x.get('Teamname') or '')]
    by_w = defaultdict(list)
    for x in rows:
        gid = (x.get('Game-ID') or '').strip()
        sid = (x.get('Spiel-ID') or '').strip()
        p = parse_gid(gid)
        if p:
            wk = p[0]
            by_w[wk].append((gid, sid, x.get('source_entry_id')))
    print('---', label, '---')

for x in leg:

import csv, os, re
from collections import defaultdict

def parse_gid(gid):
    m = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+(?:-[A-Z0-9]+)*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$', (gid or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    triple = m.group(2)
    return triple[0], triple[1], int(triple[2])

TEAM = 'BC Comet Nürnberg'
LEAGUES = {'BayL', 'BAYL'}

def count_canonical(path, label):
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        print(label, 'MISSING')
        return
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        r = csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')
        rows = [x for x in r if TEAM in (x.get('Teamname') or '')]
    by_w = defaultdict(list)
    for x in rows:
        gid = (x.get('Game-ID') or '').strip()
        sid = (x.get('Spiel-ID') or '').strip()
        p = parse_gid(gid)
        if p:
            wk = p[0]
            by_w[wk].append((gid, sid, x.get('source_entry_id')))
    print('---', label, '---')
    for w in sorted(by_w.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
        gids = set(t[0] for t in by_w[w])
        print('  parsed_week_digit=%r: rows=%s distinct_Game-ID=%s' % (w, len(by_w[w]), len(gids)))

count_canonical('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv', 'merged_gf_canonical')

path = 'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/legacy_out/latest.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    r = csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')
    leg = [x for x in r if TEAM in (x.get('Team') or '') and (x.get('League') or '') in LEAGUES]
by_w = defaultdict(set)
for x in leg:
    wk = (x.get('Week') or '').strip()
    if (x.get('Player') or '').strip() == 'Team Total':
        rn = (x.get('Round Number') or '').strip()
        mn = (x.get('Match Number') or '').strip()
        by_w[wk].add((rn, mn))
print('--- legacy latest (Team Total rows = matches) ---')
for w in sorted(by_w.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
    print('  Week=%r: matches=%s' % (w, len(by_w[w])))
"

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json, os, re, glob
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'
BAYL = re.compile(r'^BAYL', re.I)

def week_from_gid(g57):
    # same as parse_game_id first digit of triple after league prefix
    m = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+(?:-[A-Z0-9]+)*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(2)[0]

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
print('form_92 json files:', len(files))

all_games = {}  # gid -> set of source files
by_week = {}
for fp in files:
    try:
        data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    except Exception as e:
        print('skip', fp, e)
        continue
    entries = data.get('entries') or []
    for e in entries:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t or not BAYL.match(g57):
            continue
        wk = week_from_gid(g57)
        if wk not in ('3', '4'):
            continue
        all_games.setdefault(g57, set()).add(fp.name)
        by_week.setdefault(wk, set()).add(g57)

for w in ('3', '4'):
    gids = by_week.get(w, set())
    print('incoming union week', w, 'distinct BAYL Game-IDs (field 57):', len(gids))
    for g in sorted(gids):
        print(' ', g)

print('total distinct games week 3+4 in incoming:', len(all_games))
"
</summary>

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json, os, re, glob
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'
BAYL = re.compile(r'^BAYL', re.I)

def week_from_gid(g57):
    # same as parse_game_id first digit of triple after league prefix
    m = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+(?:-[A-Z0-9]+)*)[_-](\d{3})(?:_.+)?$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(2)[0]

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
print('form_92 json files:', len(files))

all_games = {}  # gid -> set of source files
by_week = {}
for fp in files:
    try:
        data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    except Exception as e:
        print('skip', fp, e)
        continue
    entries = data.get('entries') or []
    for e in entries:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t or not BAYL.match(g57):
            continue
        wk = week_from_gid(g57)
        if wk not in ('3', '4'):
            continue
        all_games.setdefault(g57, set()).add(fp.name)
        by_week.setdefault(wk, set()).add(g57)

for w in ('3', '4'):
    gids = by_week.get(w, set())
    print('incoming union week', w, 'distinct BAYL Game-IDs (field 57):', len(gids))
    for g in sorted(gids):
        print(' ', g)

print('total distinct games week 3+4 in incoming:', len(all_games))
"

```

incoming union week 3 distinct BAYL Game-IDs (field 57): 0

total distinct games week 3+4 in incoming: 0

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The Game-ID `BAYL-M-641` has a triple value of `641`. The first digit of the Game-ID triple, such as `6` in `BAYL-M-641`, represents the **Spieltag** (match day) number, not "Week" as shown in the app's Week column. The app's **Week** column in legacy systems derives its value from `parse_game_id`, which uses the first digit of the three-digit suffix as the week number  meaning it reflects the **Spieltag number**.

When users refer to "Week 4", they are referring to **Spieltag 4**, which corresponds to Game-IDs with a triple starting with `4`, such as BAYL-M-4xx.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json, re
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'

def spieltag_from_g57(g57):
    m = re.match(r'^BAYL-M-(\d)(\d)(\d)$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(1)  # first digit = Spieltag in legacy Week

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
by_st = {}
all_games = {}
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    for e in data.get('entries') or []:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        st = spieltag_from_g57(g57)
        if st is None:
            continue
        all_games.setdefault(g57, set()).add(fp.name)
        by_st.setdefault(st, set()).add(g57)

for st in sorted(by_st.keys(), key=int):
    print('Spieltag (legacy Week)=', st, 'distinct Game-IDs in incoming form_92 union:', len(by_st[st]))

# focus 3 and 4
for st in ('3', '4'):
    gids = sorted(by_st.get(st, []))
    print()
    print('Spieltag', st, 'games:', len(gids))
    for g in gids:
        print(' ', g)
"

import json, re
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'

def spieltag_from_g57(g57):
    m = re.match(r'^BAYL-M-(\d)(\d)(\d)$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(1)  # first digit = Spieltag in legacy Week

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
by_st = {}
all_games = {}
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    for e in data.get('entries') or []:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        st = spieltag_from_g57(g57)
        if st is None:
            continue
        all_games.setdefault(g57, set()).add(fp.name)
        by_st.setdefault(st, set()).add(g57)

for st in sorted(by_st.keys(), key=int):
    print('Spieltag (legacy Week)=', st, 'distinct Game-IDs in incoming form_92 union:', len(by_st[st]))

# focus 3 and 4
for st in ('3', '4'):
    gids = sorted(by_st.get(st, []))
    print()
    print('Spieltag', st, 'games:', len(gids))
    for g in gids:
        print(' ', g)
"

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'
incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
g57s = Counter()
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    for e in data.get('entries') or []:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        g57s[g57] += 1
print('distinct field-57 for Comet entries in form_92 incoming:', len(g57s))
for g, c in g57s.most_common(40):
    print(repr(g), c)
"

import json
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'
incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_92*.json'))
g57s = Counter()
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    for e in data.get('entries') or []:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        g57s[g57] += 1
print('distinct field-57 for Comet entries in form_92 incoming:', len(g57s))
for g, c in g57s.most_common(40):
    print(repr(g), c)
"

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json, re
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'

def spieltag_from_g57(g57):
    m = re.match(r'^BAYL-M-(\d)(\d)(\d)$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(1)

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_95*.json'))
print('form_95 json files:', len(files))
by_st = {}
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    entries = data.get('entries')
    if entries is None and 'total_count' in data:
        entries = data.get('entries', [])
    if entries is None:
        # page response shape
        entries = data.get('entries', [])
    if not isinstance(entries, list):
        continue
    for e in entries:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        st = spieltag_from_g57(g57)
        if st is None:
            continue
        by_st.setdefault(st, set()).add(g57)

for st in sorted(by_st.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
    print('Spieltag', st, 'distinct BAYL-M games (Comet as team field 3):', len(by_st[st]))

for st in ('3', '4'):
    print()
    print('--- Spieltag', st, '---')
    for g in sorted(by_st.get(st, [])):
        print(' ', g)
"
</summary>

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json, re
from pathlib import Path

TEAM_SUB = 'Comet'

def spieltag_from_g57(g57):
    m = re.match(r'^BAYL-M-(\d)(\d)(\d)$', (g57 or '').strip())
    if not m: return None
    return m.group(1)

incoming_dir = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
files = list(incoming_dir.glob('form_95*.json'))
print('form_95 json files:', len(files))
by_st = {}
for fp in files:
    data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    entries = data.get('entries')
    if entries is None and 'total_count' in data:
        entries = data.get('entries', [])
    if entries is None:
        # page response shape
        entries = data.get('entries', [])
    if not isinstance(entries, list):
        continue
    for e in entries:
        t = (e.get('3') or '')
        g57 = (e.get('57') or '').strip()
        if TEAM_SUB not in t:
            continue
        st = spieltag_from_g57(g57)
        if st is None:
            continue
        by_st.setdefault(st, set()).add(g57)

for st in sorted(by_st.keys(), key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 99):
    print('Spieltag', st, 'distinct BAYL-M games (Comet as team field 3):', len(by_st[st]))

for st in ('3', '4'):
    print()
    print('--- Spieltag', st, '---')
    for g in sorted(by_st.get(st, [])):
        print(' ', g)
"

```

Spieltag 2 distinct BAYL-M games (Comet as team field 3): 9
Spieltag 3 distinct BAYL-M games (Comet as team field 3): 9
Spieltag 4 distinct BAYL-M games (Comet as team field 3): 9

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Critical finding: Incoming form_95 JSON contains 9 distinct games for Comet Spieltag 3 and Spieltag 4, with Comet listed as the team in field "3". Between the fetched data and the merged canonical, two games disappeared for Spieltag 3 and one game for Spieltag 4.

This occurs during **dedupe_source_rows**, which merges entries by (Game-ID, Teamname) and retains the earliest timestamp.

If an older entry has incorrect data and a newer batch includes correct data with a later timestamp, the newer row gets discarded. The deduplication logic keeps the earliest timestamp, which can cause newer corrections to be discarded. This "first write wins" approach may lead to losing updated data if a later batch contains corrected information for the same game.

- Current logic: `if current_ts < existing_ts`, then replace
- Result: Keeps minimum (earliest) timestamp
- If prior has T1 and new batch has T2 > T1, prior is kept and update discarded
- Risk: Corrected data lost if same game/key but different timestamps</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

    return m.group(1) if m else None

path = 'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = [r for r in csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';') if TEAM == (r.get('Teamname') or '').strip()]
by = {}
for r in rows:

    st = st_gid(g)
    if st:
        by.setdefault(st, set()).add(g)
for st in ('3','4'):

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv, re
TEAM = 'BC Comet Nürnberg'

def st_gid(g):
    m = re.match(r'^BAYL-M-(\d)(\d)(\d)$', (g or '').strip())
    return m.group(1) if m else None

path = 'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = [r for r in csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';') if TEAM == (r.get('Teamname') or '').strip()]
by = {}
for r in rows:
    g = (r.get('Game-ID') or '').strip()
    st = st_gid(g)
    if st:
        by.setdefault(st, set()).add(g)
for st in ('3','4'):
    gids = sorted(by.get(st, []))
    print('merged Spieltag', st, 'count', len(gids))
    print(gids)
"

```

For BAYL-M-333, the merged row shows Teamname = Schanzer Ingolstadt 1 and opponent = Comet. Missing rows appear to be the "home" or alternate perspective where Comet is listed as the teamname, while the pipeline seems to store only one GF entry per match (from one team's perspective). For legacy conversion, each source row is expanded into 4 player lines plus a team total, suggesting both perspectives should be present. 

- For BAYL-M-333: Teamname = Schanzer Ingolstadt 1 and opponent = Comet
- For BAYL-M-325: Only Rimpar appears as team perspective
- For BAYL-M-415: Donaubowler is shown as teamname with Comet as opponent, but needs Comet as teamname row

The pipeline likely stores only one GF entry per match (from one team's perspective), but users expect both perspectives to be represented. If only Rimpar's perspective is available, Comet still appears as **opponent**, and the legacy system might still generate lines for Comet from that row.

The function `convert_source_rows_to_legacy` processes each canonical row to output data for **both** teams, not just the teamname side.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
28628 has an earlier date_updated time of 09:48:21 compared to 28808, which shows 09:48:27. **dedupe_source_rows** retains the earliest timestamp for each (Game-ID, Teamname) combination.

For the key (BAYL-M-325, BC Comet Nrnberg):
- Row 28628 has timestamp 09:48:21
- Any later row with the same key would be discarded

If there's a duplicate (325, Comet) in merged processing, only row 28628 should remain.

However, there may be a different issue: if prior data contained (325, Comet) from 28628 and batch data also includes (325, Comet), the earlier entry should be kept.

There is no Comet row in the merged result. So either 28628 was never included in the combined dataset, or it was removed during deduplication due to a matching key with an empty team value.

Another potential dedupe issue involves (BAYL-M-325, BC Comet Nrnberg) and its duplicate entry.

Checking if 28628 and 28808 might share the same dedupe key due to an adapter bug:

- staging_row_to_canonical_gf_results_v1 maps field "3" to "Teamname"
- 28628 has Teamname BC Comet Nrnberg
- 28808 has Teamname SG DJK Rimpar 1
These are different keys.

Hypothesis: Incremental ingest where `last_seen_date_updated` after initial pages prevents inclusion of page 5 in subsequent increments.</details></think>

            "text": "        page_timings: List[float] = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "        page = 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "        while True:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "            t0 = perf_counter()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "            current = self.fetch_entries_page("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "                form_id, page=page, page_size=page_size, field_ids=field_ids, sort_desc_by_updated=True"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "            page_timings.append(perf_counter() - t0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "            if not current.entries:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "                break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "            stop = False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "            for e in current.entries:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "                du = str(e.get(\"date_updated\") or \"\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "                if du and since_date_updated:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "                    try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "                        if _dt(du) \u003c= _dt(since_date_updated):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "                            stop = True"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "                            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "                    except ValueError:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "                        pass"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "                out.append(e)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "            if stop or len(current.entries) \u003c page_size:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "                break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "            page += 1"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "        return IncrementalFetchResult(entries=out, page_timings_seconds=page_timings)"
          }
        ],

          },
          {

          },

            "text": "                since_date_updated=form_state.last_seen_date_updated,"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "                field_ids=field_ids,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "            page_timings_seconds = incr.page_timings_seconds"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "        seconds_new_data_probe = float(page_timings_seconds[0]) if page_timings_seconds else 0.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "        seconds_all_entry_fetch = float(sum(page_timings_seconds))"

          {
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208,
            "text": "        t_sync = perf_counter()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 209,
            "text": "        batch_id = f\"{run_id}_form_{form_id}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 210,

          },

          },
          {

            "text": "        sanitized_csv = paths.sanitized / f\"form_{form_id}__{run_id}__sanitized.csv\""
          },

          {

            "text": "        write_csv(sanitized_csv, sanitized_rows)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 217
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 218,
            "text": "        latest = _max_date_updated(incoming_entries)"

          },

            "text": "            form_state.last_seen_date_updated = latest"

          {
            "lineNumber": 221,
            "text": "        if incoming_entries:"
          },
          {

            "text": "            form_state.last_seen_entry_id = str(max((int(str(e.get(\"id\") or \"0\")) for e in incoming_entries), default=0))"

          {

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "def run_ingest("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "    config: GfConfig,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "    mode: str = \"incremental\","
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "    field_ids: str = \"\","
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    skip_legacy: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": ") -\u003e Dict[str, object]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "    paths = PipelinePaths.default()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "    paths.ensure()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "    state = StateStore(paths.state)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "    client = GfClient(config)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "    forms = config.forms or []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "    run_id = utc_slug()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "    result: Dict[str, object] = {\"run_id\": run_id, \"mode\": mode, \"forms\": []}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "    totals_probe = 0.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "    totals_fetch = 0.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "    totals_sync = 0.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "    for form_id in forms:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "        form_state = state.load(form_id)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "        incoming_entries: List[Dict[str, str]] = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "        page_timings_seconds: List[float] = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "        if mode == \"full\" or not form_state.last_seen_date_updated:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "            page = 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "            while True:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "                t_http = perf_counter()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "                page_res = client.fetch_entries_page("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "                    form_id=form_id, page=page, page_size=config.page_size, field_ids=field_ids"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "                )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "                page_timings_seconds.append(perf_counter() - t_http)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "                raw_path = paths.incoming / f\"form_{form_id}__{run_id}__page_{page}.json\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "                write_json(raw_path, page_res.payload)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "                if not page_res.entries:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "                    break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "                incoming_entries.extend(page_res.entries)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "                if len(page_res.entries) \u003c config.page_size:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "                    break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "                page += 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "            form_state.last_full_sync_utc = state.now_utc()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "            incr = client.fetch_incremental_entries"
          }
        ],

          },

    {

          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "class GfClient:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "@staticmethod"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "def _normalize_site_base(raw: str) -\u003e str:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "        return clean"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "    @staticmethod"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    def _extract_entries(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "        entries = payload.get(\"entries\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "        if isinstance(entries, list):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "            return entries"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "        if isinstance(entries, dict):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "            return [v for v in entries.values() if isinstance(v, dict)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "        if isinstance(payload, dict):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "            vals = list(payload.values())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "            if vals and all(isinstance(v, dict) for v in vals):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "                return vals"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "        return []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "    def fetch_forms(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        return self._request_json(\"forms\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "    def fetch_entries_page("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "        form_id: int,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "        page: int,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "        page_size: int,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "        field_ids: str = \"\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "        sort_desc_by_updated: bool = True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e PageResult:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "        params: Dict[str, Any] = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "            \"form_ids\": str(form_id),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "            \"paging[page_size]\": str(page_size),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "            \"paging[current_page]\": str(page),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "        if field_ids.strip():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "            params[\"_field_ids\"] = field_ids.strip()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "        if sort_desc_by_updated:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "            params[\"sorting[key]\"] = \"date_updated\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "            params[\"sorting[direction]\"] = \"DESC\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "        payload = self._request_json(\"entries\", params=params)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "        return PageResult("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "            page=page,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "            url=f\"/entries?page={page}\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "            payload=payload,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "            entries=self._extract_entries(payload),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "    def fetch_incremental_entries("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "        self, form_id: int, since_date_updated: str, page_size: int, field_ids: str = \"\""
          },
          {

            "text": "    ) -\u003e IncrementalFetchResult:"
