```json
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Gravity Forms Data Pipeline Implementation Plan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "## Implementation status (rolling)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "| Phase (see below) | Status | Notes |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "|-------------------|--------|--------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "| Phase 0–1 | **Done** | Directories, GF client, state, &#96;incoming/&#96; + &#96;staging/&#96; |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "| Phase 2 (legacy) | **Done** | Shared &#96;gf_results_v1_adapter.py&#96; → &#96;canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv&#96; → &#96;legacy_out/&#96;; CLI &#96;run_gf_pipeline.py&#96;; refresh-from-merge on incremental no-op; &#96;rebuild_form92_legacy_from_staging.py&#96; |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "| Phase 3 (points + validation) | **Not started** | Sprint 3 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "| Phase 4 (schedule + manual API) | **Not started** | Sprint 3 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "| Phase 5 (events) | **Optional** | After Sprint 3 if needed |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "## Goal"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "Build a robust ingestion pipeline in &#96;league_analyzer_v1&#96; that:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Pulls Bowling Bayern source data from Gravity Forms REST API."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Supports full sync, incremental sync (up to timestamp), and event-driven delta ingestion."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- Stages raw incoming data before transformations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- Applies existing sanitization and conversion logic to legacy-compatible CSV."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- Recomputes points by league season."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- Stores all intermediate and final CSV artifacts in a dedicated directory tree."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- Supports scheduled polling, daily forced full sync, and manual trigger (API + frontend button)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "## Scope and Non-Goals"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "### In Scope"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "- GF REST pull jobs for selected forms."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "- Change detection using remote timestamps + persisted watermarks."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "- Staging area and deterministic transformation pipeline."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "- Operational triggers: scheduler + forced daily + manual."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "- Auditability and observability."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "### Out of Scope (for now)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- Replacing legacy CSV consumer in &#96;league_analyzer_v1&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "- Final relational UUID-native production model (future relaunch)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "- Complex bidirectional sync back to Gravity Forms."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "## Use Cases to Implement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "1. **Batch dump from GF REST**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "   - For selected forms."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "   - Modes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "     - Full dump."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "     - Incremental dump up to/after a timestamp."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "2. **Delta ingestion from GF events**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "   - Receive event notifications (webhook/plugin hook) from GF environment."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 56
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\league_analyzer_v1\\docs\\GF_DATA_PIPELINE_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.27709949016571045
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "### Behaviour"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "For each **form_id** in &#96;--forms&#96; (positive integer), unless &#96;--skip-legacy&#96;:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "1. **Map** staging → canonical via **&#96;gf_results_v1_adapter&#96;** (shared numeric field map)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "2. **Write** per-run slice: &#96;sanitized/form_\u003cid\u003e__\u003crun_id\u003e__canonical.csv&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "3. **Merge** into &#96;canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv&#96;, **dedupe** with &#96;database/conversion/bowlingbayern_legacy_core.dedupe_source_rows&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "4. **Write** &#96;legacy_out/bowling_legacy__\u003crun_id\u003e.csv&#96; (when new entries were fetched) and always refresh &#96;legacy_out/latest.csv&#96; when merge output is non-empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "**Incremental run with zero new entries:** merge file is **not** updated from that form’s batch. If **&#96;merged_gf_canonical.csv&#96;** (or legacy &#96;merged_form_92.csv&#96; for migration) **already has rows**, legacy is still **regenerated** so &#96;latest.csv&#96; stays aligned (&#96;refreshed_from_merged_only&#96;). If there is **no** merge data yet, legacy is skipped (&#96;no_new_entries_no_merged_canonical&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "### CLI"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "From repository root:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "python \"league_analyzer_v1/scripts/run_gf_pipeline.py\" --mode incremental --site \"https://bowlingbayern.de\" --ck \"ck_...\" --cs \"cs_...\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "If you omit &#96;--forms&#96;, the CLI uses the default Bowling Bayern form id set in &#96;pipeline/bowling_bayern_gf_forms.py&#96; (currently **21, 92, 95, 100, 102, 105, 113, 115, 117**). Override with &#96;--forms 92,95&#96; or env **&#96;GF_FORMS=92,95&#96;**."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "Options:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "| Flag | Purpose |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "|------|--------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "| &#96;--mode full&#96; | Full paginated pull |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "| &#96;--forms&#96; | Comma-separated ids; omit for default set (same **v1** results field map for all) |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "| &#96;--field-ids \"...\"&#96; | GF &#96;_field_ids&#96; subset |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "| &#96;--page-size 100&#96; | Page size (max 200) |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "| &#96;--insecure&#96; | Disable TLS verify (temporary only) |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "| &#96;--skip-legacy&#96; | Ingest/stage/sanitize only; no canonical merge or legacy CSV |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "### Backfill without calling GF"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "If you have &#96;staging/form_92__*_staging.csv&#96; but no merge/legacy yet (or an empty incremental staging file should not win):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "python \"league_analyzer_v1/scripts/rebuild_form92_legacy_from_staging.py\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "Loads **all non-empty** &#96;staging/form_*__*_staging.csv&#96; files, maps each through **v1** results adapter, rebuilds &#96;canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv&#96;, and writes &#96;legacy_out/latest.csv&#96; plus a timestamped &#96;bowling_legacy__\u003crun_id\u003e__from_staging.csv&#96;."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 77
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 35
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\league_analyzer_v1\\docs\\GF_PIPELINE_SPRINT1_RUNBOOK.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2589566111564636
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "2. **Delta ingestion from GF events**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "   - Receive event notifications (webhook/plugin hook) from GF environment."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "   - Pull changed entries and apply same staging/transform pipeline."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "3. **Operational control**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "   - Parameterized interval polling job."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "   - Forced full sync once daily."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "   - Manual sync trigger:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "     - Backend endpoint."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "     - Frontend button calling that endpoint."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "## Proposed Directory Layout (inside &#96;league_analyzer_v1&#96;)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "Use a dedicated root to keep artifacts isolated and auditable:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "&#96;database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "- &#96;incoming/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "  - Raw pulls from GF API (JSON and/or CSV export snapshots)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "  - Suggested naming: &#96;form_\u003cid\u003e__\u003cUTC_ISO\u003e__\u003cmode\u003e.json&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "- &#96;staging/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "  - Normalized tabular CSV per form and batch."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "  - Minimal schema aligned to current input mapping."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "- &#96;sanitized/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "  - Deduplicated/canonicalized CSV (current \"hacky\" cleanup logic formalized)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "- &#96;legacy_out/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "  - Final legacy format CSV artifacts."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "  - Includes \"latest\" and timestamped snapshots."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "- &#96;state/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "  - Watermarks/checkpoints per form."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "  - Last successful run metadata."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "- &#96;logs/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "  - Structured job logs / run manifests."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "- &#96;failed/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "  - Quarantined payloads and transform errors."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "## Pipeline Stages"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "### Stage 1: Extract (GF API client)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "- Input config:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "  - Site base URL."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "  - Consumer key/secret."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "  - Form IDs to track."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "  - Optional &#96;_field_ids&#96; subset per form."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "- Fetch modes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "  - &#96;full&#96;: pull all pages."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "  - &#96;incremental&#96;: pull entries by search filter on &#96;date_updated&#96; (or fallback logic)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "  - &#96;event&#96;: pull by specific entry IDs from incoming event payload."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "- Persist raw response per page/batch under &#96;incoming/&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "### Stage 2: Normalize to Staging"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "- Convert GF payload shapes into deterministic tabular rows."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "- Keep source columns required by mapping."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "- Include ingestion metadata columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "  - &#96;source_form_id&#96;, &#96;source_entry_id&#96;, &#96;ingested_at_utc&#96;, &#96;batch_id&#96;, &#96;source_date_updated&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "- Write to &#96;staging/&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "### Stage 3: Sanitize"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "Apply existing rules (currently in converter logic) in a reusable module:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 121
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 54
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\league_analyzer_v1\\docs\\GF_DATA_PIPELINE_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2263599932193756
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "def main() -\u003e int:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Run Sprint-1 GF ingestion pipeline\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--mode\", choices=(\"incremental\", \"full\"), default=\"incremental\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "        \"--forms\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "        default=\"\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "        help=("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "            \"Comma-separated form IDs. If omitted, uses GF_FORMS env or the default Bowling Bayern \""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "            \"set in pipeline/bowling_bayern_gf_forms.py\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "        ),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--site\", required=False, help=\"WordPress site base URL, e.g. https://bowlingbayern.de\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--ck\", required=False, help=\"GF consumer key\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--cs\", required=False, help=\"GF consumer secret\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--field-ids\", default=\"\", help=\"Optional _field_ids subset\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--page-size\", type=int, default=100)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument(\"--insecure\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Disable TLS verification\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    parser.add_argument("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        \"--skip-legacy\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "        action=\"store_true\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "        help=\"Do not merge canonical rows or write legacy CSV (Sprint 2 step)\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    args = parser.parse_args()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    _add_repo_root_to_path()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    from pipeline.config import GfConfig"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "    from pipeline.runner import run_ingest"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    site = args.site or os.getenv(\"GF_SITE_BASE_URL\", \"\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    ck = args.ck or os.getenv(\"GF_CONSUMER_KEY\", \"\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "    cs = args.cs or os.getenv(\"GF_CONSUMER_SECRET\", \"\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "    if not site or not ck or not cs:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "        print(\"Missing credentials/site. Provide --site --ck --cs or set GF_SITE_BASE_URL/GF_CONSUMER_KEY/GF_CONSUMER_SECRET.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "        return 2"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    forms_raw = (args.forms or \"\").strip() or os.getenv(\"GF_FORMS\", \"\").strip()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    if forms_raw:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "        forms = [int(x.strip()) for x in forms_raw.split(\",\") if x.strip()]"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 79
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 37,
            "line": 12
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\league_analyzer_v1\\scripts\\run_gf_pipeline.py",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "def run_ingest("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "    config: GfConfig,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "    mode: str = \"incremental\","
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "    field_ids: str = \"\","
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    skip_legacy: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": ") -\u003e Dict[str, object]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 233
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 235,
            "text": "        if not skip_legacy and has_legacy_adapter(form_id):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 236,
            "text": "            form_result[\"legacy\"] = _run_gf_canonical_legacy("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 237,
            "text": "                paths, run_id, form_id, staged_rows, len(incoming_entries)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 238,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 239,
            "text": "        elif not skip_legacy:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 240,
            "text": "            form_result[\"legacy\"] = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 241,
            "text": "                \"legacy_skipped\": True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 242,
            "text": "                \"reason\": \"invalid_form_id_for_legacy\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 244,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 245,
            "text": "            form_result[\"legacy\"] = {\"legacy_skipped\": True, \"reason\": \"skip_legacy_flag\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 246
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 247,
            "text": "        seconds_table_sync = perf_counter() - t_sync"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 248,
            "text": "        legacy_info = form_result.get(\"legacy\") or {}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 249,
            "text": "        bench_meta = _benchmark_metadata(config, mode, form_id)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 250,
            "text": "        bench_meta[\"pages_fetched\"] = len(page_timings_seconds)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 251,
            "text": "        bench_meta[\"legacy_skipped\"] = bool(legacy_info.get(\"legacy_skipped\"))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 252,
            "text": "        if legacy_info.get(\"merged_row_count\") is not None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 253,
            "text": "            bench_meta[\"merged_row_count\"] = legacy_info.get(\"merged_row_count\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 254,
            "text": "        if legacy_info.get(\"legacy_row_count\") is not None:"
          },
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I discovered critical incremental logic in `gf_client`. When `date_updated` is less than or equal to `since_date_updated`, the loop sets `stop = True` and skips the entry without appending it to the output list. When processing entries sorted by `date_updated` in descending order, the loop continues appending entries while `date_updated > since_date_updated`. Upon encountering the first entry where `date_updated <= since_date_updated`, it sets `stop = True` and skips that entry. Since the list is sorted descending, all subsequent entries in the same page will also be older, so skipping them prevents any further appending from that page.

- Loop skips entries when `date_updated <= since_date_updated`
- Stops adding entries once first older entry is found
- All remaining entries in current page are also older due to descending sort order
- Breaks to next page logic after finding first older entry The bug involves entries with equal `date_updated` values, where ordering among duplicates may not be consistent. For issue 28628, if the first pipeline run was incremental using `last_seen` from another machine or default state, entire old pages could be missed. Alternatively, a full sync might paginate until all entries are fetched, but only if the page is full. It's unlikely the GF API omits entries during full sync due to sorting. A hypothesis is that the merged prior data came from an initial subset that excluded rows from page 5.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
There's a bug in incremental fetch! Waterline sets `last_seen_date_updated` to the maximum date from the previous run's incoming entries. During incremental runs, entries with a `date_updated` less than or equal to the `since_date_updated` are skipped, meaning only entries strictly newer than the watermark are added. This causes issues when entries have identical timestampsthose from the previous run may be missed if their exact timestamp matches the watermark, even if they were not included in the current batch. For example, entry 28628 has a timestamp of 09:48:21, while 28808 has 09:48:27, so they differ, but identical timestamps could lead to missing data. So if the watermark advances past 09:48:27, incremental fetch returns entries updated **after** that timestamp. Entry 28628 wouldn't returncorrectly for incrementalif it was already included in a prior run.

The issue is that 28628 was never included in the prior run, so it's missed due to incorrect logic.

How could the **first full merge** miss 28628?

- Theory: Multiple forms or a single run with form 95 containing thousands of entries
- The `incoming_entries` from one full run includes all pages
- Page 5 must be included in the batch
- If `page_size` is 200 and there are 72 entries for form 92, but form 95 has thousands, then max pages need to be calculated correctly</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is a concise report of what we verified on your machine.

## 1. Are the games in the fetched data?

**Yes — for BayL they live on form 95, not form 92.**  
On-disk `incoming/form_95*.json` contains **nine** distinct `BAYL-M-*` matches for **BC Comet Nürnberg** as **field `"3"` (Teamname)** for both **Spieltag 3** and **Spieltag 4** (your “Week 3 / Week 4” in the legacy `Week` column = that first digit after `BAYL-M-`).

For the three matches that look “short” in the app:

| Match (`57`) | Comet row in GF | Entry id (from incoming) |
|----------------|-----------------|---------------------------|
| `BAYL-M-325` | `_Li` side, `"3": "BC Comet Nürnberg"` | **28628** |
| `BAYL-M-333` | `_Li` side | **28625** |
| `BAYL-M-415` | `_Li` side | **28729** |

The opponent `_Re` rows for the same games are present too (e.g. **28808** for Rimpar on `BAYL-M-325`).

So the **missing lines are specifically the Comet `_Li` Gravity Forms entries**, not “missing from the website” in the snapshots you have under `incoming/`.

## 2. Where they disappear

They **do not** appear in `canonical/merged_gf_canonical.csv` (no `source_entry_id` **28625**, **28628**, **28729** in that file).  
They **do** appear for the same `Game-ID` with the **other** team in field `"3"` (e.g. only **28808** for `BAYL-M-325` with `Teamname` = SG DJK Rimpar 1).

`dedupe_source_rows` in `bowlingbayern_legacy_core.py` keys on **`(Game-ID, Teamname)`**, so Rimpar and Comet are **different** keys — **this is not** “two matches collapsed into one row” for the same team.

So the loss is **before** that dedupe logic, in practice: **those rows never made it into the merged file in any run that built `merged_gf_canonical.csv`**, while older **saved** JSON pages still contain them. Typical causes that fit the evidence:

- **Incremental runs** only append entries with `date_updated` **strictly after** the stored watermark (`gf_client.fetch_incremental_entries` skips `date_updated <= last_seen_date_updated`). If Comet’s `_Li` rows were **never** merged into `prior`, and later only the `_Re` side (or other entries) keep moving the watermark, those `_Li` rows may **never** be seen again by incremental fetch.
- A **later full sync** pulls **current** GF data; your **historical** `incoming/*.json` can still list entries that are **no longer** returned by the API, so “on disk yes, in latest merge no” is still consistent.

Staging/sanitize are not the split point for legacy merge: **`_run_gf_canonical_legacy` uses raw `staged_rows`**, not `sanitize_rows` output.

## 3. Match to your counts (4×7 / 4×8)

For **Comet as `Teamname`**, `merged_gf_canonical.csv` has **7** distinct `Game-ID`s for Spieltag **3** and **8** for Spieltag **4** — same as **Team Total** match counts in `legacy_out/latest.csv`. The three missing Comet-side games above account for **2 + 1** missing matches vs nine.

---

**Practical next step:** run a **full** ingest (`run_gf_pipeline` / your CLI with **full** mode) so the merge sees **all** current GF entries again; if those `_Li` entries still exist remotely, they should land in `merged_gf_canonical.csv`. If they were removed or never returned by the API, you’d need a **one-off rebuild** from a complete snapshot (e.g. union of known-good `incoming` + merge) — that would be a small follow-up change/script if you want it automated.

I did not change any code in this step; this was tracing only. If you want, we can add a guardrail (e.g. periodic forced full sync, or rebuild-from-disk) in a follow-up.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see a critical issue: The pipeline loops over multiple forms. For each form, the pipeline:
- Reads the current merged file (including outputs from previous forms in the same run)
- Appends only that form's canonical batch
- Deduplicates and overwrites the merged file

This causes issues when processing multiple forms because earlier forms' data gets overwritten by later forms' data during deduplication, leading to loss of data from earlier forms in the same run. The bug occurs because the pipeline processes forms sequentially, but each form reads the merged file fresh from disk after the previous form writes to it. This means that when processing form 21, it merges its batch with the global prior data, then writes the result. Later, when processing form 95, it again reads the merged file fresh, which now includes form 21's data from this run, along with older global data. This causes incorrect merging behavior where earlier forms' data can be lost or improperly handled during deduplication.

The sequence of events:
- Form 1: Merges prior_global + batch1  writes merged
- Form 2: Reads merged (includes form1's data) + batch2  writes merged
- Form 95: Reads merged (includes form1 and form2 data from this run) + batch95  writes merged

This leads to data loss because earlier forms' data gets overwritten during deduplication in subsequent iterations The bug may stem from how `dedupe_source_rows` handles timestamps, keeping the earliest entry per (Game-ID, Teamname). If prior data has a row A with timestamp T1 and canonical_batch has row B with an earlier timestamp T2, then row B would overwrite row A during deduplication. However, if prior doesnt contain (325, Comet) but canonical_batch does, the row is correctly added.

- Prior data includes all merged entries from previous forms in the same run
- Canonical batch only includes this runs staged rows for the current form
- Deduplication logic may incorrectly overwrite earlier entries due to timestamp comparison</details></think>

Here is what actually happened on your repo for the full sync you ran.

## Full sync did run and did rewrite `latest.csv`

The run log `league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/logs/run_20260412T200955Z.json` shows **`mode": "full"`**, form **95** fetched **540** entries over **6** pages, and legacy wrote `legacy_out/latest.csv` with **`legacy_row_count": 8890`** (same count as after forms 21 and 92 in that same run). So the pipeline completed; the output did not change in **shape** (row count) because the **merged canonical row count stayed 1778** end-to-end.

## Why Comet still “looks wrong”: those rows are not on the API anymore

For the missing Comet `_Li` games we cared about (e.g. GF entry **28628** for `BAYL-M-325`), the **current** full-sync artifacts **do not contain that entry at all**:

- No `28628` (nor `28625` / `28729`) in  
  `staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv`
- No `28628` in any  
  `incoming/form_95__20260412T200955Z__page_*.json`

So **`--mode full` only pulls what Gravity Forms returns today**. Your older `incoming/form_95__20260411*.json` snapshots still list those ids, but the **20260412T200955Z** fetch does not. In that situation, merge + legacy **cannot** add lines to `latest.csv` for those games’ Comet-as-`Teamname` rows, and the file will look unchanged aside from possible minor edits elsewhere.

Typical GF-side explanations: entries removed, trashed, superseded by import workflow, or otherwise no longer returned by `GET /entries` for that form.

## What you can do next

1. **In WordPress / Gravity Forms**: confirm whether entries **28628**, **28625**, **28729** still exist (and are active, not in trash). If they were deleted, only restoring them (or re-importing) fixes the source.
2. **Backfill from your own disk** (if you must not lose history): a one-off merge from archived `incoming/*.json` into canonical would be a **separate** tool/workflow—the stock pipeline only trusts the live API for each run’s `staged_rows`.

**Note:** `sanitized_rows` (514) vs `staged_rows` (540) for form 95 only affects the **sanitized CSV**; legacy merge uses **un-sanitized** `staged_rows`, so that 26-row difference is **not** why Comet is short.

If you want, we can add a small `scripts/` utility that rebuilds `merged_gf_canonical.csv` from a glob of saved `incoming` JSONs (with clear warnings about duplicates and timestamps).

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from collections import defaultdict
path = r'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
print('rows', len(rows))
# sanitize key logic
empty_gid = 0
empty_eid = 0
by_key = defaultdict(list)
for i, r in enumerate(rows):
    game_id = (r.get('Game-ID') or r.get('Spiel-ID') or '').strip()
    team = (r.get('Teamname') or '').strip()
    eid = (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip()
    if not game_id:
        empty_gid += 1
    if not eid:
        empty_eid += 1
    key = (game_id, team, eid)
    by_key[key].append((i, r.get('id'), r.get('57'), r.get('3'), r.get('source_date_updated')))
print('empty Game-ID/Spiel-ID cols', empty_gid, 'of', len(rows))
print('empty source_entry_id', empty_eid)
print('unique sanitize keys', len(by_key))
multi = {k:v for k,v in by_key.items() if len(v)>1}
print('keys with >1 row', len(multi))
for k, v in list(multi.items())[:5]:
    print('  key', k, 'count', len(v), 'sample ids', [x[1] for x in v[:5]])
# rows that would be dropped: all but first kept per key (sanitize keeps earliest ts)
# simulate
def sim(rows):
    dedup = {}
    for raw in rows:
        row = {k: (v.strip() if isinstance(v,str) else v) for k,v in raw.items()}
        game_id = row.get('Game-ID') or row.get('Spiel-ID') or ''
        team = row.get('Teamname') or ''
        eid = row.get('source_entry_id') or ''
        key = (game_id, team, eid)
        existing = dedup.get(key)
        if existing is None:
            dedup[key] = row
            continue
        ex_ts = existing.get('source_date_updated', '')
        cur_ts = row.get('source_date_updated', '')
        if cur_ts and (not ex_ts or cur_ts < ex_ts):
            dedup[key] = row
    return dedup
d = sim(rows)
print('after sanitize count', len(d), 'dropped', len(rows)-len(d))
"

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from collections import defaultdict
path = r'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
print('rows', len(rows))
# sanitize key logic
empty_gid = 0
empty_eid = 0
by_key = defaultdict(list)
for i, r in enumerate(rows):
    game_id = (r.get('Game-ID') or r.get('Spiel-ID') or '').strip()
    team = (r.get('Teamname') or '').strip()
    eid = (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip()
    if not game_id:
        empty_gid += 1
    if not eid:
        empty_eid += 1
    key = (game_id, team, eid)
    by_key[key].append((i, r.get('id'), r.get('57'), r.get('3'), r.get('source_date_updated')))
print('empty Game-ID/Spiel-ID cols', empty_gid, 'of', len(rows))
print('empty source_entry_id', empty_eid)
print('unique sanitize keys', len(by_key))
multi = {k:v for k,v in by_key.items() if len(v)>1}
print('keys with >1 row', len(multi))
for k, v in list(multi.items())[:5]:
    print('  key', k, 'count', len(v), 'sample ids', [x[1] for x in v[:5]])
# rows that would be dropped: all but first kept per key (sanitize keeps earliest ts)
# simulate
def sim(rows):
    dedup = {}
    for raw in rows:
        row = {k: (v.strip() if isinstance(v,str) else v) for k,v in raw.items()}
        game_id = row.get('Game-ID') or row.get('Spiel-ID') or ''
        team = row.get('Teamname') or ''
        eid = row.get('source_entry_id') or ''
        key = (game_id, team, eid)
        existing = dedup.get(key)
        if existing is None:
            dedup[key] = row
            continue
        ex_ts = existing.get('source_date_updated', '')
        cur_ts = row.get('source_date_updated', '')
        if cur_ts and (not ex_ts or cur_ts < ex_ts):
            dedup[key] = row
    return dedup
d = sim(rows)
print('after sanitize count', len(d), 'dropped', len(rows)-len(d))
"
```

import csv
from collections import defaultdict
path = r'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
by_eid = defaultdict(list)
for i, r in enumerate(rows):
    key = (r.get('Game-ID') or r.get('Spiel-ID') or '', r.get('Teamname') or '', r.get('source_entry_id') or '')

    idxs = dups[eid]
    r0, r1 = rows[idxs[0]], rows[idxs[1]]
    same = all(r0.get(k)==r1.get(k) for k in r0.keys())
    print(eid, 'rows', len(idxs), 'identical' if same else 'DIFFERENT', '57=', r0.get('57'), '3=', (r0.get('3') or '')[:40])
"

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Entry 28637 appears on both page 5 and page 6 during the same full sync run. That's a **pagination overlap bug** - either the GF API returns overlapping pages when sorted by date_updated DESC, or our pagination termination is incorrect.

The full sync loop continues while receiving full pages of 100 entries. If page 5 has 100 entries and page 6 has 40 but overlaps with page 5's last entries, this would cause duplicate entries to be retrieved. The GF API paging may be unstable when many entries share identical `date_updated`, causing unpredictable sort order ties and shifting page boundaries.

- Deduplicate `incoming_entries` by entry `id` before staging in runner.py as a clean, minimal fix.
- Fix sanitize to use fields "57"/"3"/"1" when Game-ID/Teamname are missing, improving dedupe semantics, though duplicate entry_ids still require handling by same key (game, team, entry). If using 57+3+entry_id, identical rows will collapse to one entry. For duplicate rows with the same entry ID (though shouldn't happen), deduplicating by entry ID is the safest approach.

A report will be written in markdown format.

Deduplication can be added in runner before staging using a set to track seen IDs and preserving only the first occurrence.

This deduplication logic should be implemented in `run_ingest` after the extend loop for full mode, and also for incremental mode, although overlap issues are less likely there. The best fix is to deduplicate incoming entries by ID after collecting all pages before `rows_from_entries`. A helper function `_dedupe_entries_by_id` filters out duplicates based on entry ID, keeping only the first occurrence. If an entry lacks an ID, it should still be kept, but only if it's unique by index or other criteria.

- Deduplicate by entry ID, keeping the first occurrence.
- Entries without an ID are retained if they dont duplicate previous entries.
- This logic applies to both full and incremental sync modes. - Deduplicate entries by ID, skipping duplicates and keeping the first occurrence.
- Entries without an ID are retained if they dont duplicate previous entries.
- A helper function `_dedupe_entries_by_id` will be added to runner.py to handle deduplication before staging.
- Sanitize logic will be updated to use GF numeric keys (fields "57"/"3"/"1") for game/team when building keys, ensuring correct deduplication even if entry IDs are missing.
- A markdown report will be generated, and the fix will be implemented as described.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Identifying the issue: `sanitize_rows` uses `Game-ID`/`Teamname`, but GF staging uses `57`/`3`, so every row's dedupe key becomes `("", "", source_entry_id)`. Duplicate GF entry IDs from pagination overlap (same entry on pages 5 and 6) collide and one copy is dropped. Implementing entry-level dedupe after fetch and aligning sanitize keys with GF field IDs.

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is the report and what we changed.

## What happens between staged and sanitized

`sanitize_rows` trims rows and deduplicates by  
`(Game-ID or Spiel-ID, Teamname, source_entry_id)`, keeping the row with the **earliest** `source_date_updated` when keys collide.

GF staging CSVs do **not** use the names `Game-ID` / `Teamname` / `Spiel-ID`. They use Gravity Forms field ids: **`57`** (game id), **`3`** (team), **`1`** (Spiel label). Until now, `sanitize_rows` only looked at `Game-ID` / `Spiel-ID` / `Teamname`, so for form 95 every row had:

- `game_id == ""`
- `team == ""`
- `entry_id == source_entry_id` (still correct)

So the dedupe key was effectively **`("", "", "<entry id>")`**.

## Which rows were dropped (your `20260412T200955Z` run)

There were **26** keys with **two identical rows** (same `source_entry_id`, same payload). Sanitize kept one copy each → **540 → 514**.

Those **26** GF entry ids (each appeared **twice** in `staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv`):

`28637`, `28638`, `28639`, `28640`, `28641`, `28642`, `28643`, `28644`, `28645`, `28646`, `28744`, `28745`, `28746`, `28747`, `28832`, `28834`, `28835`, `28836`, `28837`, `28838`, `28839`, `28840`, `28841`, `28842`, `28844`, `28942`

**Why:** the same entry id appears on **two different pages** of the same full sync (example: **`28637`** is in both `incoming/form_95__20260412T200955Z__page_5.json` and `...__page_6.json`). Paging is sorted by `date_updated` DESC; when many rows share the same timestamp, the boundary between pages can shift and **Gravity Forms can return overlaps**. Concatenating pages without deduping produces duplicate rows; sanitize then removes the duplicate keys.

So the **540** you see is **rows returned** (with overlaps). **Distinct entry ids in that staging file are 514**, which matches `sanitized_rows` in the run log.

**Important:** Legacy merge uses **`staged_rows`**, not `sanitize_rows`, so this sanitize step did **not** cause the missing Comet `_Li` rows in `latest.csv` — that was already established as absent from the live API response.

## Code updates (for correctness and stable counts)

1. **`runner.py`** — after each fetch, entries are deduped by GF **`id`** before staging (`_dedupe_entries_by_id`), so staging matches distinct entries and you do not rely on sanitize to fix pagination overlap.

2. **`sanitize.py`** — dedupe now falls back to GF field ids **`57` / `1`** for game and **`3`** for team when canonical column names are missing, so sanitize matches the real staging schema and would only collapse rows that truly share the same game + team + entry id.

If you want a one-off **human-readable report file** (CSV of dropped duplicate pairs), say so and we can add a small script; the list above is the full set for that run.

---

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv, json
from pathlib import Path

def staging_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    ids = [r.get('source_entry_id') or r.get('id') for r in rows]
    u = set(i for i in ids if i)
    dups = len(rows) - len(u)
    return len(rows), len(u), dups, u

def incoming_union_form95(pattern):
    u = set()
    total = 0
    for fp in sorted(Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming').glob(pattern)):
        data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
        for e in data.get('entries') or []:
            if str(e.get('form_id')) != '95':
                continue
            total += 1
            u.add(str(e.get('id') or ''))
    u.discard('')
    return total, len(u), u

# recent full sync staging
p_new = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv')
r_new, u_new, d_new, set_new = staging_ids(p_new)
print('NEW staging', p_new.name, 'rows', r_new, 'unique', u_new, 'row-unique', d_new)

# older staging files form_95
for p in sorted(Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging').glob('form_95__*__staging.csv')):
    if p.name == p_new.name:
        continue
    r, u, d, s = staging_ids(p)
    if r == 0:
        continue
    print('OLD staging', p.name, 'rows', r, 'unique', u, 'row-unique', d)

# union all incoming form_95 from April 11 run (single run id)
for run in ['20260411T140132Z', '20260411T143200Z', '20260412T194729Z']:
    tot, nu, s = incoming_union_form95(f'form_95__{run}__page_*.json')
    if tot:
        print('INCOMING union', run, 'row pulls', tot, 'unique ids', nu)

# set diff: ids in older union but not in new staging
old = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260411T140132Z__staging.csv')
if old.is_file():
    _, _, _, set_old = staging_ids(old)
    missing_in_new = set_old - set_new
    new_only = set_new - set_old
    print()
    print('Compare 20260411T140132Z staging vs 20260412T200955Z')
    print('  ids in OLD not in NEW:', len(missing_in_new))
    print('  ids in NEW not in OLD:', len(new_only))
    if missing_in_new:
        print('  sample missing', sorted(missing_in_new, key=int)[:15])
    if new_only:
        print('  sample newonly', sorted(new_only, key=int)[:15])
"
</summary>

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv, json
from pathlib import Path

def staging_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    ids = [r.get('source_entry_id') or r.get('id') for r in rows]
    u = set(i for i in ids if i)
    dups = len(rows) - len(u)
    return len(rows), len(u), dups, u

def incoming_union_form95(pattern):
    u = set()
    total = 0
    for fp in sorted(Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming').glob(pattern)):
        data = json.loads(fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
        for e in data.get('entries') or []:
            if str(e.get('form_id')) != '95':
                continue
            total += 1
            u.add(str(e.get('id') or ''))
    u.discard('')
    return total, len(u), u

# recent full sync staging
p_new = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv')
r_new, u_new, d_new, set_new = staging_ids(p_new)
print('NEW staging', p_new.name, 'rows', r_new, 'unique', u_new, 'row-unique', d_new)

# older staging files form_95
for p in sorted(Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging').glob('form_95__*__staging.csv')):
    if p.name == p_new.name:
        continue
    r, u, d, s = staging_ids(p)
    if r == 0:
        continue
    print('OLD staging', p.name, 'rows', r, 'unique', u, 'row-unique', d)

# union all incoming form_95 from April 11 run (single run id)
for run in ['20260411T140132Z', '20260411T143200Z', '20260412T194729Z']:
    tot, nu, s = incoming_union_form95(f'form_95__{run}__page_*.json')
    if tot:
        print('INCOMING union', run, 'row pulls', tot, 'unique ids', nu)

# set diff: ids in older union but not in new staging
old = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260411T140132Z__staging.csv')
if old.is_file():
    _, _, _, set_old = staging_ids(old)
    missing_in_new = set_old - set_new
    new_only = set_new - set_old
    print()
    print('Compare 20260411T140132Z staging vs 20260412T200955Z')
    print('  ids in OLD not in NEW:', len(missing_in_new))
    print('  ids in NEW not in OLD:', len(new_only))
    if missing_in_new:
        print('  sample missing', sorted(missing_in_new, key=int)[:15])
    if new_only:
        print('  sample newonly', sorted(new_only, key=int)[:15])
"
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter

def dup_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    c = Counter(r.get('source_entry_id') or '' for r in rows)
    return {eid for eid, n in c.items() if eid and n > 1}

def ids_set(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    return {r.get('source_entry_id') or '' for r in rows if r.get('source_entry_id')}

p_old = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260411T140132Z__staging.csv')
p_new = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv')
old, new = ids_set(p_old), ids_set(p_new)
missing = old - new
dups = dup_ids(p_new)
print('missing old->new count', len(missing))
print('dup id count', len(dups))
print('intersection missing AND dup', len(missing & dups))
print('missing not dup', sorted(missing - dups, key=int)[:30])
print('dup not missing', sorted(dups - missing, key=int)[:30])
"
</summary>

```bash
cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter

def dup_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    c = Counter(r.get('source_entry_id') or '' for r in rows)
    return {eid for eid, n in c.items() if eid and n > 1}

def ids_set(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    return {r.get('source_entry_id') or '' for r in rows if r.get('source_entry_id')}

p_old = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260411T140132Z__staging.csv')
p_new = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv')
old, new = ids_set(p_old), ids_set(p_new)
missing = old - new
dups = dup_ids(p_new)
print('missing old->new count', len(missing))
print('dup id count', len(dups))
print('intersection missing AND dup', len(missing & dups))
print('missing not dup', sorted(missing - dups, key=int)[:30])
print('dup not missing', sorted(dups - missing, key=int)[:30])
"
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Your skepticism is justified. Here’s a tighter account that separates **arithmetic**, **pagination**, and **what changed in GF**.

## The “26” is not a lucky accident

For any fetch with **R** rows and **U** distinct `source_entry_id` values:

- **R − U** = number of “extra” rows beyond one row per id → those rows can only be **second (or further) copies** of ids that already appear at least once.

If the form really has **T = 540** active entries and this response has **R = 540** rows and **U = 514** distinct ids, then:

- **T − U = 540 − 514 = 26** entries never appear **as a row at all** in this response.
- **R − U = 540 − 514 = 26** rows are **duplicate lines** for some of the **514** ids that *do* appear.

So **T = R** implies **missing distinct count = duplicate-row count**. That is algebra, not two unrelated effects that “happen” to both be 26.

So: **540 rows with 514 unique does *not* mean “only pagination overlap”.** It is consistent with **both** “some ids returned twice” **and** “some ids not returned at all”, with the **same** gap size when R equals the expected total.

## What we can show from your repo (two different groups of 26)

Using `form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv` vs `form_95__20260411T140132Z__staging.csv`:

1. **Pagination-style duplicates (26 ids)**  
   Each of these appears **twice** in the 540-row file (same id, same payload), and the same id shows up on **two different pages** of the same run (e.g. **28637** on both `page_5` and `page_6`). That set is:

   `28637` … `28646`, `28744`–`28747`, `28832`, `28834`–`28842`, `28844`, `28942`

2. **Ids present in the older staging snapshot but not in the new unique set (26 ids)**  
   These are **not** the duplicate-id set; the intersection of the two sets is **empty**. This group includes the Comet `_Li` ids we cared about (**28625**, **28628**, **28729**, …).

So in your data, **“26 duplicates” and “26 ids lost vs older snapshot” are different 26-element sets** — your instinct that it’s not “just pagination explaining everything” is correct. Pagination explains **why some ids appear twice**; it does **not** explain **why Comet’s `_Li` rows vanished**; that’s **GF/API drift** (removed, trashed, import changed, etc.).

## Older “full” logs don’t contradict this

Several older form 95 stagings also show **540 rows / 514 unique / 26 duplicate lines** (`20260412T182851Z`, `194101Z`, `194729Z`, same as `200955Z`). So the duplicate pattern is **stable across runs**, not a one-off.

After **deduping by GF `id` before staging**, you already have a run with **514 rows and 514 unique** (`form_95__20260412T202008Z__staging.csv`) — i.e. **one row per distinct entry** with **no** duplicate lines. That matches “514 distinct entries represented in that fetch”, not “540 with weird double-counting only”.

## How this relates to GF’s `"entries": "540"`

That field is **Gravity Forms’ notion of how many entries exist for the form** (often all-time / admin count). It does not guarantee that a **single REST paged pull** returns 540 **distinct** rows without overlap or without entries missing from the current API surface. Aligning **540** with **what REST returns** still requires checking **distinct ids** and **whether R = T** for that pull.

---

**Bottom line:** I should not have framed it as “540 is only overlaps”; the safer statement is: **540 rows with 514 unique implies 26 duplicate rows among returned ids; if you also believe there are 540 distinct entries in GF, the same numbers imply 26 entries never appear in that response.** Your repo additionally shows **26 ids dropped vs an older snapshot** that are **not** the same 26 as the duplicate-id set — so **two separate issues share the number 26 only when T=R, but the *sets* are different.**

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from collections import defaultdict
path = r'league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
by_eid = defaultdict(list)
for i, r in enumerate(rows):
    eid = (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip()
    if eid:
        by_eid[eid].append((i, r))

dups = {e: v for e, v in by_eid.items() if len(v) > 1}
print('duplicate entry ids:', len(dups))
identical = 0
diff_keys_summary = []
for eid, pairs in sorted(dups.items(), key=lambda x: int(x[0])):
    (i0, r0), (i1, r1) = pairs[0], pairs[1]
    if len(pairs) != 2:
        print('WARN', eid, 'count', len(pairs))
    diffs = [k for k in r0.keys() if (r0.get(k) or '') != (r1.get(k) or '')]
    if not diffs:
        identical += 1
    else:
        diff_keys_summary.append((eid, diffs[:20], len(diffs)))

print('pairs with all columns equal:', identical)
print('pairs with any column difference:', len(diff_keys_summary))
for item in diff_keys_summary[:10]:
    print('  eid', item[0], 'diff_col_count', item[2], 'sample cols', item[1])
"
</summary>

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
path = r'tmp/liga_bayl-m_ergebnisse-2026-04-12.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
print('data rows', len(rows))
# find entry id column - German export
keys = list(rows[0].keys()) if rows else []
id_col = None
for k in keys:
    if 'Eintrags-ID' in k or k.strip() == 'Eintrags-ID':
        id_col = k
        break
if not id_col:
    # try exact
    for k in keys:
        if 'ID' in k and 'Eintrag' in k:
            id_col = k
            break
print('id column repr:', repr(id_col))
ids = [(r.get(id_col) or '').strip() for r in rows]
id_counts = Counter(i for i in ids if i)
multi = [eid for eid, c in id_counts.items() if c > 1]
print('unique Eintrags-ID', len(id_counts))
print('duplicate id values (>1 row):', len(multi))
if multi:
    by = defaultdict(list)
    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
        eid = (r.get(id_col) or '').strip()
        if eid in multi:
            by[eid].append((i, r))
    identical_n = 0
    diff_n = 0
    diff_examples = []
    for eid in sorted(multi, key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 0):
        pairs = by[eid]
        if len(pairs) != 2:
            print('  id', eid, 'appears', len(pairs), 'times')
        r0, r1 = pairs[0][1], pairs[1][1]
        diffs = [k for k in r0.keys() if (r0.get(k) or '') != (r1.get(k) or '')]
        if not diffs:
            identical_n += 1
        else:
            diff_n += 1
            if len(diff_examples) < 5:
                diff_examples.append((eid, diffs[:12], len(diffs)))
    print('duplicate pairs with ALL columns equal:', identical_n)
    print('duplicate pairs with ANY column difference:', diff_n)
    for ex in diff_examples:
        print('  example eid', ex[0], 'n_diff_cols', ex[2], 'cols', ex[1])
# also check duplicate by (Game-ID, Teamname) if no id dup
gid_col = 'Game-ID' if rows and 'Game-ID' in rows[0] else None
team_col = 'Teamname' if rows and 'Teamname' in rows[0] else None
if gid_col and team_col:
    gt = Counter(((r.get(gid_col) or '').strip(), (r.get(team_col) or '').strip()) for r in rows)
    gt_multi = [k for k, c in gt.items() if c > 1 and k[0]]
    print('duplicate (Game-ID, Teamname) keys:', len(gt_multi))
"
</summary>

```bash
import csv
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
path = r'tmp/liga_bayl-m_ergebnisse-2026-04-12.csv'
with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
print('data rows', len(rows))
# find entry id column - German export
keys = list(rows[0].keys()) if rows else []
id_col = None
for k in keys:
    if 'Eintrags-ID' in k or k.strip() == 'Eintrags-ID':
        id_col = k
        break
if not id_col:
    # try exact
    for k in keys:
        if 'ID' in k and 'Eintrag' in k:
            id_col = k
            break
print('id column repr:', repr(id_col))
ids = [(r.get(id_col) or '').strip() for r in rows]
id_counts = Counter(i for i in ids if i)
multi = [eid for eid, c in id_counts.items() if c > 1]
print('unique Eintrags-ID', len(id_counts))
print('duplicate id values (>1 row):', len(multi))
if multi:
    by = defaultdict(list)
    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
        eid = (r.get(id_col) or '').strip()
        if eid in multi:
            by[eid].append((i, r))
    identical_n = 0
    diff_n = 0
    diff_examples = []
    for eid in sorted(multi, key=lambda x: int(x) if x.isdigit() else 0):
        pairs = by[eid]
        if len(pairs) != 2:
            print('  id', eid, 'appears', len(pairs), 'times')
        r0, r1 = pairs[0][1], pairs[1][1]
        diffs = [k for k in r0.keys() if (r0.get(k) or '') != (r1.get(k) or '')]
        if not diffs:
            identical_n += 1
        else:
            diff_n += 1
            if len(diff_examples) < 5:
                diff_examples.append((eid, diffs[:12], len(diffs)))
    print('duplicate pairs with ALL columns equal:', identical_n)
    print('duplicate pairs with ANY column difference:', diff_n)
    for ex in diff_examples:
        print('  example eid', ex[0], 'n_diff_cols', ex[2], 'cols', ex[1])
# also check duplicate by (Game-ID, Teamname) if no id dup
gid_col = 'Game-ID' if rows and 'Game-ID' in rows[0] else None
team_col = 'Teamname' if rows and 'Teamname' in rows[0] else None
if gid_col and team_col:
    gt = Counter(((r.get(gid_col) or '').strip(), (r.get(team_col) or '').strip()) for r in rows)
    gt_multi = [k for k, c in gt.items() if c > 1 and k[0]]
    print('duplicate (Game-ID, Teamname) keys:', len(gt_multi))
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import csv
from pathlib import Path

def load_csv_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
    col = 'Eintrags-ID'
    return { (r.get(col) or '').strip() for r in rows if (r.get(col) or '').strip() }, rows

def load_staging_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    return { (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip() for r in rows if (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip() }

csv_path = Path('tmp/liga_bayl-m_ergebnisse-2026-04-12.csv')
staging_path = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv')

csv_ids, csv_rows = load_csv_ids(csv_path)
api_ids = load_staging_ids(staging_path)

missing_in_api = sorted(csv_ids - api_ids, key=int)
only_in_api = sorted(api_ids - csv_ids, key=int)

print('CSV unique Eintrags-ID:', len(csv_ids))
print('API unique source_entry_id (staging 200955Z):', len(api_ids))
print('In CSV but NOT in API set:', len(missing_in_api))
print('In API but NOT in CSV set:', len(only_in_api))
print()
print('Missing in API (sorted):', missing_in_api)
print()
print('Only in API (first 30):', only_in_api[:30], '... total', len(only_in_api))

# Pattern: contiguous runs
def runs(ids):
    ids = sorted(int(x) for x in ids)
    if not ids:
        return []
    out = [[ids[0], ids[0]]]
    for x in ids[1:]:
        if x == out[-1][1] + 1:
            out[-1][1] = x
        else:
            out.append([x, x])
    return out

print()
print('Missing IDs as contiguous ranges:', runs(missing_in_api))

# Optional: date_updated from CSV for missing rows
with open(csv_path, newline='', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
by_id = { (r.get('Eintrags-ID') or '').strip(): r for r in rows }
print()
print('Per missing row: Game-ID, Teamname (short), Aktualisierungsdatum')
for eid in missing_in_api[:30]:
    r = by_id.get(eid, {})
    print(eid, (r.get('Game-ID') or '')[:14], ((r.get('Teamname') or '')[:28]).ljust(28), r.get('Aktualisierungsdatum', '')[:19])
if len(missing_in_api) > 30:
    print('...')
"

```bash
from pathlib import Path

def load_csv_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
    col = 'Eintrags-ID'
    return { (r.get(col) or '').strip() for r in rows if (r.get(col) or '').strip() }, rows

def load_staging_ids(path):
    with open(path, newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        rows = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    return { (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip() for r in rows if (r.get('source_entry_id') or '').strip() }

csv_path = Path('tmp/liga_bayl-m_ergebnisse-2026-04-12.csv')

only_in_api = sorted(api_ids - csv_ids, key=int)

print('API unique source_entry_id (staging 200955Z):', len(api_ids))

print('Only in API (first 30):', only_in_api[:30], '... total', len(only_in_api))

# Pattern: contiguous runs
def runs(ids):
    ids = sorted(int(x) for x in ids)
    if not ids:
        return []
    out = [[ids[0], ids[0]]]
    for x in ids[1:]:
        if x == out[-1][1] + 1:

        else:
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is the crosscheck using `tmp/liga_bayl-m_ergebnisse-2026-04-12.csv` as the reference set and `staging/form_95__20260412T200955Z__staging.csv` as the REST snapshot (unique `source_entry_id`).

## Counts

| Source | Unique entry IDs |
|--------|-------------------|
| CSV export | **540** |
| API staging (that run) | **514** |
| In CSV **but not** in API | **26** |
| In API **but not** in CSV | **0** |

So the API set is a **strict subset** of the export by id: nothing extra, **26 ids never appear** in any page of that fetch.

## The 26 missing ids

`28619`, `28624`–`28632`, `28729`–`28731`, `28756`, `28816`, `28819`, `28822`–`28830`, `28944`

## Patterns

**1. Gravity Forms insert order (blocks of consecutive `Eintrags-ID`)**  
Several runs of consecutive numbers, e.g. **`28624`–`28632`** (9 in a row), **`28729`–`28731`**, **`28822`–`28830`**. That usually means **one import / one batch** wrote those rows back-to-back.

**2. Shared `Aktualisierungsdatum` (to the second)**  
From the CSV, every missing row uses one of only **four** timestamps on **2026-02-15**:

- `09:48:21` — bulk of the block (incl. `28619`, `28624`–`28632`)  
- `09:48:25` — `28729`–`28731`, `28756`  
- `09:48:28` — `28816`, `28819`, `28822`–`28830`  
- `09:48:32` — `28944`  

So the gap is **not** random noise: it lines up with **tight time buckets** from a single historical import wave.

**3. `Spiel-ID` suffix is *not* “only `_Li`”**  
Among the 26, **14** end with `_Li` and **12** with `_Re`. So you **cannot** explain the API hole as “REST drops all `_Li` rows”; it drops a **mixed** `_Li`/`_Re` slice that still clusters by **id + timestamp**.

**4. Games / teams**  
The list includes your known Comet `_Li` rows (`28625`, `28628`, `28729`) but also many other teams and both perspectives; it is **one batch-shaped hole** in the REST listing, not a Comet-only rule.

---

**Interpretation (careful):** the shape (consecutive ids + identical `date_updated` seconds + mixed `_Re`/`_Li`) fits **“REST `/entries` listing under `date_updated DESC` paging misses a slice of rows that share the same second with many neighbors”** more than “export invents rows.” The export is consistent with **540 real rows**; the client fetch for that run **failed to surface 26 of those ids at all**, while **26 other ids appeared twice** on concatenated pages (you already proved those duplicates are byte-identical).

So the actionable pattern is: **same-second import cohort + paging**, not “540 = 514 + 26 duplicates only” as the full story—the **26 missing** and **26 duplicate lines** are separate symptoms that **line up in count** when the export total is 540 and the fetch returns 540 lines with 514 distinct ids.

---

_**User**_

so, do we have a paga by page log prior to merging? if so, are  the entries missign there already? if so, should we experiment with page sizes?

---

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "c:\Users\cfell\OneDrive\Documents\001_repositories\league_analyzer"; py -3 -c "
import json
from pathlib import Path
missing = {'28619', '28624', '28625', '28626', '28627', '28628', '28629', '28630', '28631', '28632', '28729', '28730', '28731', '28756', '28816', '28819', '28822', '28823', '28824', '28825', '28826', '28827', '28828', '28829', '28830', '28944'}
run = '20260412T200955Z'
incoming = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
pages = sorted(incoming.glob(f'form_95__{run}__page_*.json'))
print('pages', [p.name for p in pages])
found = {eid: [] for eid in missing}
page_counts = []
for p in pages:
    data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    entries = data.get('entries') or []
    page_counts.append((p.name, len(entries)))
    ids = {str(e.get('id')) for e in entries}
    for eid in missing:
        if eid in ids:
            found[eid].append(p.name)
print('page entry counts:', page_counts)
print()
not_any = [eid for eid in sorted(missing, key=int) if not found[eid]]
on_pages = {eid: found[eid] for eid in missing if found[eid]}
print('missing ids found on at least one page:', len(on_pages))
if on_pages:
    for k, v in sorted(on_pages.items(), key=lambda x: int(x[0])):
        print(' ', k, v)
print('missing ids on NO page:', len(not_any))
print(not_any[:15], '...' if len(not_any)>15 else '')
"

```bash
import json
from pathlib import Path
missing = {'28619', '28624', '28625', '28626', '28627', '28628', '28629', '28630', '28631', '28632', '28729', '28730', '28731', '28756', '28816', '28819', '28822', '28823', '28824', '28825', '28826', '28827', '28828', '28829', '28830', '28944'}
run = '20260412T200955Z'
incoming = Path('league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming')
pages = sorted(incoming.glob(f'form_95__{run}__page_*.json'))
print('pages', [p.name for p in pages])
found = {eid: [] for eid in missing}
page_counts = []
for p in pages:
    data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    entries = data.get('entries') or []
    page_counts.append((p.name, len(entries)))
    ids = {str(e.get('id')) for e in entries}
    for eid in missing:
        if eid in ids:
            found[eid].append(p.name)
print('page entry counts:', page_counts)
print()
not_any = [eid for eid in sorted(missing, key=int) if not found[eid]]
on_pages = {eid: found[eid] for eid in missing if found[eid]}
print('missing ids found on at least one page:', len(on_pages))
if on_pages:
    for k, v in sorted(on_pages.items(), key=lambda x: int(x[0])):
        print(' ', k, v)
print('missing ids on NO page:', len(not_any))
print(not_any[:15], '...' if len(not_any)>15 else '')
"
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Clear answer:

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what the repo actually gives you and what it implies.

## Per-page log **before** merge

Yes. On a **full** ingest, each HTTP page is written as its own file:

`league_analyzer_v1/database/pipeline/bowling_bayern/incoming/form_95__<run_id>__page_<n>.json`

For `20260412T200955Z` there are **6** files (`page_1` … `page_6`), with **100 / 100 / 100 / 100 / 100 / 40** entries respectively. That is the raw GF REST payload **before** `extend`, dedupe, staging, or canonical merge.

There is also a run summary: `logs/run_<run_id>.json` (counts, paths), but the **page-level truth** is those `incoming/...__page_*.json` files.

## Are the “missing vs CSV” ids already missing there?

For that run: **yes, all of them.**

I scanned every `form_95__20260412T200955Z__page_*.json` for the **26** `Eintrags-ID` values that are in your export but not in API staging. **None** of those ids appear on **any** page. So they are **not** dropped later in staging/sanitize/merge; they **never show up** in the REST listing for that fetch.

(That matches the earlier grep: no `28628` in that run’s incoming glob.)

## Should you experiment with page size?

**Worth trying as an experiment**, with the right expectation:

- **Page size** (and anything that changes sort / page boundaries) can change **which** rows overlap at page edges (the **duplicate** rows we saw). So e.g. `--page-size 50` or `200` might reduce or reshuffle overlaps.
- It **cannot** pull in ids that the API never returns. For this run, the 26 missing ids are **absent from every page**, so **page size alone will not fix that subset** unless Gravity Forms’ behavior is weirder than “stable omission” (e.g. a bug that only manifests at certain `page_size` + `sorting[key]=date_updated` combinations). That’s empirical: run another full sync with a different `--page-size` and diff `Eintrags-ID` sets against your CSV again.

**Other experiments** (if the API allows them): different `sorting` (e.g. ascending `date_updated`, or sort by `id`), or a second pass with a different window—anything that forces a **total order** distinct from “`date_updated` DESC only” can reduce “same second, unstable order” effects.

**Practical takeaway:** keep using **`incoming/...__page_*.json`** as the ground truth for “what the API returned this run”; compare that set of ids to your CSV to see whether a page-size or sort experiment changes the missing set.
